## Summary

Adds the next top-down deficiency item: a dedicated Privacy & Research Controls trust page.

## Why

The deficiency roadmap calls for a page explaining manuscript privacy, external research controls, whether manuscripts are used for training, user consent, and what happens with uploaded work.

## Changes

- Adds `/privacy-research-controls` as a dedicated plain-language trust page.
- Explains manuscript sovereignty and author-controlled visibility.
- Defines manuscript-first analysis and bounded external research.
- Adds research modes:
  - Manuscript-only mode
  - Bounded context mode
  - Package-preparation mode
- Explains the manuscript lifecycle:
  - Upload or paste
  - Evaluate
  - Revise
  - Export or package
- Adds guardrails against:
  - public indexing by default
  - unsupported publishing promises
  - blind rewriting
  - external research overriding manuscript evidence
  - premature film/TV/adaptation routing
- Adds a plain-language Privacy FAQ.
- Updates `/resources` so the Privacy & Research Controls card links to the new standalone page.

## Scope

Public trust copy and navigation only.

No auth, database, storage, security implementation, evaluation, report, Storygate runtime, Agent Readiness runtime, or pipeline behavior changed.

## Note

This is not a legal privacy policy. It is a product trust page. Formal terms/privacy policy can still be added separately.
